prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "侧弓步" (lateral lunge / side step lunge) exercise, front view, facing the viewer. Person posture: Athletic person at the bottom of a lateral lunge: feet wide apart with weight shifted entirely onto the right leg, right knee bent at 90 degrees with right thigh parallel to the floor and right foot flat on the ground; left leg fully extended straight to the side with foot flat on the ground; this is at the moment of the lunge depth, similar to T1217 lateral squat but with more dynamic step-out emphasis; hands clasped at chest height; torso upright with slight forward lean; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: quadriceps and gluteus maximus of the bent (right) leg, with adductor stretch on the extended (left) leg. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: gluteus medius and hamstring group. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "股四头肌" pointing to the quadriceps of the bent leg; "臀大肌 / 臀中肌" pointing to the glute area; "内收肌群" pointing to the adductor muscles. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
